details.text: Derivative: فَرَضْنَا – We have mandated. فَرَضَ in origin means to make holes or notches in wood. It is like the meaning of mandatory comes from the meaning of carving or etching something into wood.

- وَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ – “If it had not been for the favour of Allah to you and His mercy …”. Here the answer to this statement is omitted and can be deduced and understood from the context. In this context the omitted answer to “If it had not been for the favour of Allah to you and His mercy” could be: “by bringing these Sharee’ah rulings to deal with your affairs you would have found yourselves in dire problems” or “you would have had the set punishment for Zina imposed upon you”.
Derivative: عُصْبَةٌ a gang, band or group – people that have something in common or people that are tough and intimidating. Can refer to a muscular or social strength.
Derivative: الإِفْك from أَفَكَ which means to turn from the right direction and to deviate from the truth. It means to lie and falsify.

Derivative: أَفَضْتُمْ from أَفاضَ which means literally to pour out or forth and can refer to speech that is let out without restraint just as it can refer to what you indulge or delve into. Here it is related to the Ifk (great lie) that was allowed to spill out and was not restrained.

Derivative: أَلْسِنَة means tongues and is a part of the mouth, which suggests a lesser extent than had mouths been mentioned i.e., here the Ifk (great lie) touched upon their tongues, which was followed by the mouths (i.e., a greater extent) speaking without knowledge.

Derivative: تَلَقَّوْنَهُۥ from تَلَقَّى which means to receive and is connected to the meaning of meeting or coming in contact, whereby the meaning is that the Ifk (great lie) came into contact, met or touched upon the tongues. The meaning here can also imply to seize and grab hold of i.e., the tongues caught hold of the Ifk (great lie).
Derivative: هَيِّن is something that is easy, goes down or is accepted easily and is also something that does not require any effort at all i.e., something taken lightly.
- It means that it is a extremely huge and serious matter with Allah.

Derivative: سُبْحانَ is often used in the Qur’an in response to Shirk to state that Allah is glorified above what they associate with Him. Here it indicates to the gravity of the great crime of participation in the Ifk (great lie).
Derivative: بُهْتان is a very serious accusation and slander made against somebody.
Derivative: تَشِيعَ from شَاعَ to spread and to become common place.

Derivative: اِجْلِدُوا comes from جَلَدَ – جَلْدَة and means to flog or lash. جِلْد means skin and جَلَدَ is to whip the skin whereby the furthest extent of the injury would be to the skin. It is also said that the instrument used for the lashing is made of leather or animal hide/skin (جِلْد).
Derivative: المُنْكَر refers to evil that everyone agrees upon is evil and can also refer to the evil that was unknown, denied or unspeakable before its appearance.
Derivative: الفَحْشاء refers generally to shamelessness, lewdness, vulgarity and indecency.

Derivative: زَكَى means to purify, thrive and grow or increase. Here it refers to countering the effects of Shaytan i.e., had it not been for Allah’s favour and mercy none of you would have been able to withstand Shaytan’s influence and remain upright.
Derivative: يَأْتَلِ from اِئْتَلَى – يأتلي – ائتلاءً which means to swear or take an oath to not do something.
Derivative: سَعَةُ refers to expansiveness, wide capacity and abundance (i.e., in terms of wealth and means).
Derivative: يَعْفُو from عَفَا - عَفْوٌ to pardon or forgive. It is like مَغْفِرَة – غَفَرَ (to forgive) except it is in relation to not punishing or penalising someone i.e., to pardon.
Derivative: يَصْفَحُ means to turn the page i.e., to pardon, forgive and overlook as if you are starting from anew and the matter or act that required pardoning did not take place.
Derivative: دَين here refers to the جَزاء (recompense) i.e., they will be paid or given in full their true (or just) recompense.
Derivative: مُبَرَّءُونَ means they are absolved, dissociated and innocent (from).
Derivative: تَسْتَأْنِسُوا from اِسْتَأْنَسَ means to make yourself known by word or action in order to seek permission.
Derivative: مَتَاعٌ can mean goods or belongings but also a utility and something that you make use from or a service.

Derivative: بُيُوت means houses but it can also mean any place where the night is spent in from the verb بَاتَ which means to spend the night. Here it is talking about بُيُوت which are not resided in or specified to be resided in like those places which are for public utilities or services.

- “The male fornicator or adulterer does not marry except a female fornicator/adulterer or Mushrikah (female polytheist)” is not a command but rather a general statement of reality i.e., that this is in line with their behaviour and desires.
Derivative: يَغُضُّوا from غَضَّ means to lower and is used in relation to two things: Voices and the eyes (or gaze).

Derivative: يَصْنَعُ means to manufacture which means in a simple sense “to do or produce from your action” however, manufacturing entails more than doing in the sense that it includes the process of planning and the associated steps.
Derivative: جُيوب means chest cavities.

Derivative: خُمُر is the plural of خِمار which means veil. It has the same origin as خَمْر (alcohol or intoxicating beverage) because it covers the mind or creates a barrier between the mind and the tongue. خِمار is what covers the head and chest of a woman.
Derivative: إِرْبَة means the need for which there is no alternative and here it refers to desire for women.
Derivative: The use of يَضْرِبْنَ from the verb ضَرَبَ “to strike” indicates that the veil should be placed firmly upon their heads and chest cavity.
Derivative: الأَيامَى refers to those who are not or no longer married including widows, divorced and those who have not been able to get married yet.
Derivative: يَسْتَعْفِف - اِسْتِعْفاف means to make efforts to guard oneself.
- This expression refers to When the slave wants to pursue a contract with His owner to but His freedom.

Derivative: نُور means light and it can carry multiple meanings here in respect to the statement “Allah is the light (نُور) of the heavens and earth”. The simplest meaning is that Allah is the means (i.e., the light) by which the skies and the earth can be seen for what they truly are. When you have Iman in Allah you can understand the universe and the world around you for what it really is.
Derivative: مِصْباح (lamp) comes from صُبْح (morning) and as such it is a tool that simulates the morning by providing light.
Derivative: كَوْكَبٌ is a large star and is also used for planets.
Derivative: دُرِّىٌّ means excessively shining.

the example in this verse can be understood to be applied to the human being and the potential for his heart to be illuminated by the light. the potential is there in terms of the lamp, glass and pure oil but is yet to be realised and only needs to be lit. the light upon light expresses the realisation and materialisation of that through the fuel of guidance نُور (light) which comes from outside connecting to the pre-programmed potential with the human which is also a نُور (light) and activating it, thus becoming “light upon light”.

- “Not to the East and not to the West” means that it stands alone. If it is on the East or West of the woods the sun will only shine on one half of the tree and does not enjoy the full blessings of the light of the sun. The tree is therefore constantly under sunlight and it is these that produce the purest forms of oil.
- A blessed tree. مُّبَٰرَكَةٍ means that it gives and produces more than what is usually expected from it.

- The use of both مَثَلُ (example) and كَ (like) provides the indication that the example being provided does not perfectly encompass the نُور (light) of Allah but rather it provides a likeness in a way that we can gain some comprehension about it.

Derivative: تُرْفَعَ means to be raised or elevated: Here it relates to the بُيُوتٍ (i.e., Masajid) and the elevation can be related to their height and construction and it can also refer to their standing, prominence and being held in esteem above all impurities and bad acts.
Derivative: تَتَقَلَّبُ means to be turned over, tossed around, rattled or fluctuate.
- It literally means without counting or limits on the compensation and is an expression to mean in great abundance i.e., so generous and abundant that it can’t be quantified and beyond counting.
Derivative: سَرَاب is a mirage. شَراب in Arabic is a drink and سَرَاب is what appears to be a drink but is not. Someone believes that he has found a شَراب but discovers that it is only a سَرَاب.
Derivative: قِيعَةٍ a unique portion of land, possibly low and level.

Derivative: مُحْصَن comes from إِحْصان which means to put someone inside a fort and a woman who is inside the protection of a family household or a dignified woman is called a مُحْصَنَة because a woman being amongst her family is like the image of her being inside a fort and protected.
Derivative: يَرْمُونَ from رَمَى which means to throw. Here it means to throw an accusation.

- “(Those who) throw (يَرْمُونَ) accusations against Muhsanaat” uses language associated to war in the case where رَمَى is also used to mean to shoot arrows and مُحْصَنَة means to be protected in the fort, thereby picturing this act to be similar to an act of war: Those who shoot accusations at those protected in forts.
Derivative: ظُلُمَاتٍ refers to layers of darkness.
Derivative: لُجِّي refers to something that persists and goes on. In respect to the sea in means its depth and also that it goes on continuously in the distance where no land can be seen.

Derivative: يَفْعَلُونَ from فِعْل and refers to an act that is done subconsciously unlike an عَمَل which is a deliberate action. So, seeing is a فِعْل but looking is an عَمَل and hearing is a فِعْل whilst listening is an عَمَل. The actions and even the Tasbeeh of animals, like the birds in this verse is a فِعْل however, for us it should be an عَمَل. If we do acts of worship in a subconscious manner, we are turning what should be an عَمَل into a فِعْل.
Derivative: صافَّات means to be outstretched and refers to the wings of the bird when it glides and floats in the air and is not flapping its wings.
Derivative: رُكَامًا means layer upon layer piled up on top of each other or an accumulation of mass.
Derivative: الوَدْق is light rain or rain that is continuous whether light or heavy.

Derivative: يُزْجِى means to push something gently and with ease. It is used for things which are easy to push, in the case where the derivative مُزْجى refers to something that is little and insignificant.
- سَنَا بَرْقِهِ – Flash of lightening: it refers to a Flash of light that is so strong that for a few moments you cannot see.
- أُوْلِى الأَبْصار means possessors of vision which means possessors of insight.
Derivative: مُبَيِّنات means absolutely clear and clarifying miraculous signs.

- وَمَا أُولَٰئِكَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِينَ – In the Arabic language it is usual for the Khabar (خَبَر) to be in the Nakirah (indefinite form) however here ال (Alif Laam) is used to make it definite, which highlights that they are not “the” believers which not only negates that they are believers but also affirms the existence of true believers i.e., from the Sahabah.
Derivative: مُذْعِنِينَ from أَذْعَنَ which means to obey and submit easily and voluntarily – like camels with their heads lowered in submission.
Derivative: يَحِيفَ from حَافَ means to incline or lean against, be biased and deal unjustly.

Derivative: يَتَّقْهِ The Sukoon is placed upon the middle radical which is unusual and whenever there is an unusual change in the Harakaat it is done for the purpose of emphasising that word within the sentence. Here it emphasises a strong Taqwaa (cautiousness) of Allah where he makes great efforts to be cautious in respect to the way he conducts himself in his future.

it is recorded that ibn ‘abbas, may allah be pleased with him, said: that he fears allah (يَخْشَ اللَّهَ) in respect to what he has done of sins (in the past) and that he is cautious and heedful of allah (يَتَّقْهِ) in respect to what is to come in the future.

- طَاعَةٌ مَّعْرُوفَةٌ refers to an obedience that is expected and usual i.e., does not require a special individual request. It can also mean that their obedience is well known, in that it is restricted to the tongue but does not materialise into action. This all relates to the hypocrites.
Derivative: حُمِّلَ – What he has been charged with and been made responsible for. Literally the burdens that have been placed upon him.
Derivative: يُمَكِّنَ means to fortify and stabilise.
Derivative: اِسْتَخْلَفَ means to be left behind on the land to be in charge of it and to take care of its affairs i.e., to make them inherit the land as Khulafaa’ (successors).

Derivative: عَوْرات refers to the three times mentioned and they are called عَوْرات (‘Awraat) because these are the times when the person removes his clothing and reveals his ‘Awrah (i.e., nakedness).
Derivative: طَوَّافُون literally to go around which means to roam back and forth freely.
Derivative: مُتَبَرِّجات comes from تَبَرُّج which means to make yourself stand out, bring attention to yourself and show your beauty or charms off through make-up, jewellery and other means.

Derivative: القَواعِد literally means those women who are sitting and is the broken plural form of القَاعِدَات. It refers to those women who are passed the age of bearing children and have no desire for marriage i.e., elderly women.
- This can mean to say Salaam to those in the house when you enter and also to say Salaam to your own selves if you are the only one to enter the house.

Derivative: شَأْن (matter, affair or task) differs from أَمْرٍ (matter, affair or task) in that شَأْن refers to a matter that can only be undertaken by the original person to whom it relates to i.e., it I specific to them to deal with.
Derivative: لِواذ means a side of a mountain. It refers to a hidden or discrete place that is snuck away to.
Derivative: دُعَاء means supplication but also a call or summons to come or to do something.

Derivative: يَتَسَلَّلُونَ from تَسَلَّلَ. سَلَّ means to pull out or withdraw gently like a sword taken slowly from its sheath without making a noise. تَسَلَّلَ means to slip away bit by bit in a discrete and smooth manner.

the transition from the first person of you in the plural (أَنتُمْ) to the third person of they will be returned (يُرْجَعُونَ). first allah condemns them by stating that he knows where they stand or knows their condition and then because their position or condition is so deplorable, allah does not address them directly after that and speaks about them in the third person form as they are underserving of being spoken to in the first person.
